2604.08261.title: DBMF: A Dual-Branch Multimodal Framework for Out-of-Distribution Detection
2604.08261.paper_summary: The complex and dynamic real-world clinical environment demands reliable deep learning (DL) systems. Out-of-distribution (OOD) detection plays a critical role in enhancing the reliability and generalizability of DL models when encountering data that deviate from the training distribution, such as unseen disease cases. However, existing OOD detection methods typically rely either on a single visual modality or solely on image-text matching, failing to fully leverage multimodal information. To overcome the challenge, we propose a novel dual-branch multimodal framework by introducing a text-image branch and a vision branch. Our framework fully exploits multimodal representations to identify OOD samples through these two complementary branches. After training, we compute scores from the text-image branch ($S_t$) and vision branch ($S_v$), and integrate them to obtain the final OOD score $S$ that is compared with a threshold for OOD detection. Comprehensive experiments on publicly available endoscopic image datasets demonstrate that our proposed framework is robust across diverse backbones and improves state-of-the-art performance in OOD detection by up to 24.84%
2604.08261.paper_summary_zh: 複雜且動態的現實臨床環境要求可靠的深度學習（DL）系統。當遇到偏離訓練分佈的數據時，例如未見過的疾病案例，分佈外（OOD）檢測在增強DL模型的可靠性和泛化能力方面扮演著關鍵角色。然而，現有的OOD檢測方法通常依賴於單一的視覺模態或僅依賴於圖像-文本匹配，未能充分利用多模態信息。為了克服這一挑戰，我們提出了一個新穎的雙分支多模態框架，通過引入文本-圖像分支和視覺分支。我們的框架充分利用多模態表示，通過這兩個互補的分支來識別OOD樣本。在訓練後，我們從文本-圖像分支（$S_t$）和視覺分支（$S_v$）計算分數，並將它們整合以獲得最終的OOD分數$S$，該分數與閾值進行比較以進行OOD檢測。在公開可用的內視鏡圖像數據集上的綜合實驗表明，我們提出的框架在多種骨幹上都具有穩健性，並在OOD檢測中將最先進的性能提高了高達24.84%。
2604.08261.author: Jiangbei Yue et.al.
2604.08261.authors: Jiangbei Yue, Darren Treanor, Venkataraman Subramanian, Sharib Ali
